troubleshooting.title: Mermaid Diagrams Not Rendering in Plan Documents
troubleshooting.shortdesc: Mermaid diagram blocks in a project plan document display as raw text instead of rendered diagrams.

condition.p: In the plan document preview panel, a fenced code block tagged as mermaid is displayed as plain text rather than as a visual diagram.

cause.p: The tkinterweb library is not installed. Without tkinterweb, the plan document preview falls back to a plain-text renderer that does not execute JavaScript or Mermaid.

step.cmd: Activate the virtual environment and install tkinterweb:
Open the plan document and click Preview.
step.stepresult: Mermaid diagrams should now render in the preview panel.

cause.p: Internet access is unavailable. Mermaid rendering uses the Mermaid.js library loaded from a CDN. If the host machine has no internet access, the diagram cannot render.

step.cmd: Verify internet connectivity from the machine running Portfolio Manager.
If the machine is offline intentionally, use the plan document in edit mode. Diagrams are preserved as source text and render correctly when connectivity is restored.

p.b: The Mermaid diagram contains a syntax error.

step.cmd: Click Edit to open the plan document editor.
Review the Mermaid code block for syntax errors. Common issues include:

ul.li: Missing opening keyword (flowchart TD, sequenceDiagram, and so on)
Node labels containing unescaped special characters

step.cmd: Correct the syntax, then click Preview to verify the diagram renders.
